text:
<think>I should start by summarizing the initial number of potted plants Mary has on her window ledges.</think>
Mary has 2 potted plants on each of her 40 window ledges.
<think>Now, I should calculate the total number of potted plants on the window ledges by multiplying 2 by 40.</think>
This means she has 2 × 40 = 80 potted plants on her window ledges.
<think>I should mention the 18 new potted plants she received yesterday and add them to her total.</think>
Yesterday, she received 18 new potted plants from her favorite plant nursery.
<think>Now, I should add the 80 existing plants to the 18 new ones to get her total before giving any away.</think>
So, before giving any away, Mary has 80 + 18 = 98 potted plants.
<think>I should now state how many plants she plans to give away: 1 from each of the 40 ledges.</think>
She has decided to give 1 potted plant from each of the 40 window ledges to friends and family.
<think>I should calculate the total number of plants she will give away, which is 40.</think>
This means she will give away 40 potted plants in total.
<think>Now, I should subtract the 40 plants given away from her total of 98 to find out how many she will have left.</think>
After giving away 40 potted plants, Mary will remain with 98 - 40 = 58 potted plants.
<think>I should now present the final answer clearly after the #### as requested.</think>
#### 58
